present.title_on_plate: Proto-Nago: The First Signs of Survival
present.canon_implications: Source for data/nago_proto_signs.json; origin layer beneath the refined Nago system.
present.episode_usage: Visual reference for origin-era / pre-standardization signage.
present.alt_text: Illustrated reference plate titled Proto-Nago: The First Signs of Survival, showing fourteen hand-scratched survival marks with their meanings, the materials and surfaces they were made with, and a closing quote about why they were made.
present.tactile_notes: No tactile form documented on this plate; tactile forms for proto marks are AWAITING FRAGMENT.
open_questions: Which proto marks persisted into refined Nago and which were dropped or absorbed — not stated on either plate.

present.title_on_plate: Nago: Symbology of Umada — A Visual Language. A Survival Memory. A Promise to Understand.
present.canon_implications: Source for the eight core survival signs, seven function families, and five Visual Grammar primitives in data/nago_symbology.json and data/nago_signs.json.
present.episode_usage: Visual reference for Language & Signage and Nago Symbology sections; art direction reference for in-world signage.
present.alt_text: Illustrated reference plate titled Nago: Symbology of Umada, showing design principles, a five-symbol visual grammar, eight core survival signs with hand gestures and tactile forms, seven color-coded symbol families, examples of in-world signage, sign-combination phrase examples, a tactile-system explanation, and an honor and origin statement.
present.tactile_notes: Tactile form illustrated per core sign directly on the plate; source for written tactile-form descriptions.
open_questions: Vector/CSS-level shape spec for the Visual Grammar primitives is AWAITING a dedicated asset-extraction pass.

present.provenance: Realized animatic, UMADA_Aderin_SHIP_v1 ship package. Score: yellowbirdbeats.
present.canon_implications: Dates the Friedmandostorp blast/rescue to March 19, 2226. Confirms Ada's full credited name as Adanna "Ada" Venter. Restates ADA-1's first ethic on-screen as "Trust body over record." See 01_canon/VISUAL_CANON_REGISTRY.md for the full caption transcript and the Kassey open question.
present.episode_usage: Referenced from the Visual Archive section and the Cape Wipeout, Pt. 1 episode-release entry.
present.alt_text: Animatic video depicting the Friedmandostorp rescue sequence from Cape Wipeout, Pt. 1, dated March 19 2226, scored by yellowbirdbeats.
present.tactile_notes: Not applicable to video. An audio transcript for the score and any dialogue remains AWAITING FRAGMENT — no speech-to-text tooling was available to produce one without guessing at content.

caption_transcript.text: UMADA / Friedmandostorp / March 19, 2226 (title card over four comic-panel thumbnails; one names “Kassey”).
Corrugated roofs. Cement dust. Clay. Sewage. Bread in the road.
A face first. Then the damage.
Not dead. Not yet. (a mark resembling “FFF” is etched near the figures, unexplained)

And one name burning under all of it. (torn military record, stamped CLEARANCE DENIED: “...VENTER,” “...STED KIA,” “[C]HIMERA SURVIVOR. SCIENTIST. EMPATH.” — with “Kassey” burned across it in a different script)
The road comes broken: paperwork, fire, betrayal — and Kassey.
The record says dead. The body says no.

Talla calls. Q questions the risk. Quartz moves to carry her. (credit line at frame bottom: ADANNA “ADA” VENTER — [role descriptor removed at creator's direction, 2026-06-23], OFF T—, clipped by the source frame's right edge)

present.transcript_note: Covers on-screen captions and document/credit text only, recovered by manual frame review (ffmpeg, 2fps sampling, plus targeted high-resolution crops) during the 2026-06-22 Phase C ingestion pass. The audio track has not been reviewed.
open_questions: Kassey (resolved, Kemi 2026-06-23): a separate person — Ada's lover, a man — and the giver of her necklace, the same entity as “KC”. His name burns across Ada's record; why is left open. See 02_characters/KASSEY_DOSSIER.md.
What is the “FFF” mark in the rubble-rescue panel?
What is past “OFF T—” on Ada's credit line? Clipped by the source file's frame edge.
No transcript exists yet for the audio track (score and any dialogue).

present.provenance: AI-generated (DALL-E) concept art; prompt authored by Aderin from locked canon facts; approved by Kemi as Ada's locked reference image, 2026-06-23. Captured at 13_fragments/2026-06-23_ada_reference_portrait.md.
present.canon_implications: Locks Ada's wardrobe and gear: woodland-pattern camo uniform, name tape "ADA," gold collar rank insignia, wings/pilot-style chest badge, sleeve flag patch (design unspecified), web belt with holstered sidearm and knife, wristwatch — alongside her already-locked ponytail, necklace, slim Nigerian build, and cold-and-methodical demeanor. See 02_characters/ADA_DOSSIER.md.
present.episode_usage: Reference image for any future depiction of Ada.
present.alt_text: Painted portrait of Ada, a slim Nigerian woman in a woodland-pattern camo military uniform with a name tape reading ADA, gold collar rank insignia, a wings-style chest badge, and a sleeve flag patch, standing composed with a calm, focused expression. She wears a thin silver necklace and has her dark hair slicked back into a ponytail. A holstered sidearm and knife sit on her web belt and a wristwatch on her wrist. Behind her, a sunlit coastal African town with corrugated rooftops, a lighthouse, and a weathered signpost reading AGULHAS GUIDELIKE PUNT 34 50' S 20 00' E.
present.tactile_notes: Not applicable — static reference image, not a Nago sign.
open_questions: Does the wings/pilot-style badge indicate an aviation background, or is it decorative? See 01_canon/OPEN_QUESTIONS.md #31.
